etymology.description: Depicts a hand holding a rod or brush, similar to components seen in 事(事) (shì, "affair") and 支(支) (zhī, "support")
etymology.interpretation: The character 尹(yǐn), meaning "govern," visually represents authority through a hand grasping a tool, symbolizing control or administration. This concept aligns with governance and oversight, embodied in its depiction.

examples.chinese: 尹在这个地区掌权(尹在這個地區掌權)。
他被国王任命为尹(他被國王任命為尹)，负责管理法律事务(負責管理法律事務)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bèi guówáng rènmìng wéi yǐn, fùzé guǎnlǐ fǎlǜ shìwù.
examples.english: He was appointed as a governor by the king, responsible for managing legal affairs.

examples.chinese: 在古代，尹的职位非常重要(在古代，尹的職位非常重要)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gǔdài, yǐn de zhíwèi fēicháng zhòngyào.
examples.english: In ancient times, the position of governor was very important.
